name: Grand Chess
author: Christian Freeling (1984) / AGP port
description: Grand Chess (Christian Freeling, 1984), on a 10x10 board. Adds two compound pieces: the Marshall (rook + knight) and the Cardinal (bishop + knight). Pawns start on the third rank and may step one or two squares on their first move. There is no castling. A pawn may promote ONLY to a piece type its owner has lost (so you can never field more of a piece than you started with) — optional on the last two ranks before the end, compulsory on the final rank; if no captured piece is available, a pawn is stuck on the ninth rank. Checkmate wins; stalemate and the usual material/repetition/fifty-move situations draw.
